channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Family of a man killed by agents responding to a 911 call seeks answers
item.description: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man fatally shot by agents told NPR they want answers.
item.content:encoded: A troubling pattern of federal agents fatally shooting civilians is developing, with deaths in Maine, Texas and Tennessee. The family of a man fatally shot by agents told NPR they want answers.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Former U.S. Special Envoy to Iran on the state of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Robert Malley, a negotiator of a 2015 Iran nuclear deal, about President Trump saying the U.S. is reinstating a blockade on Iranian ports and charging a toll on cargo.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Robert Malley, a negotiator of a 2015 Iran nuclear deal, about President Trump saying the U.S. is reinstating a blockade on Iranian ports and charging a toll on cargo.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump says U.S., not Iran, will collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump says the Strait of Hormuz is open, and the U.S., not Iran, will collect tolls.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says the Strait of Hormuz is open, and the U.S., not Iran, will collect tolls.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Maine Sen. Angus King talks about fatal shooting of a man by federal agents
item.description: Sen. Angus King, I-Maine, reacts to the fatal shooting of a 26-year-old Colombian man by federal law enforcement in Biddeford, Maine, Monday morning.
item.content:encoded: Sen. Angus King, I-Maine, reacts to the fatal shooting of a 26-year-old Colombian man by federal law enforcement in Biddeford, Maine, Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.content:encoded: Trump says the U.S. will collect tolls and impose blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, man killed by federal agents in Maine, states sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros merger.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Baby Rose returns with new album, 'Yearnalism'
item.description: The sultry voice of Baby Rose returns on a new album called "Yearnalism," which she considers the study of desire.
item.content:encoded: The sultry voice of Baby Rose returns on a new album called "Yearnalism," which she considers the study of desire.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Judge says Trump IRS lawsuit was filed to 'manipulate the judicial process'
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Georgetown law professor and former federal prosecutor Paul Butler about Monday's ruling in the IRS settlement case involving President Trump.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Georgetown law professor and former federal prosecutor Paul Butler about Monday's ruling in the IRS settlement case involving President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Why a growing mass of warm water in the Pacific could be trouble for future weather
item.description: A massive heat wave in the Pacific Ocean coupled with a strong El Nino could spell trouble for extreme weather in the U.S. and also elevate sea level rise.
item.content:encoded: A massive heat wave in the Pacific Ocean coupled with a strong El Nino could spell trouble for extreme weather in the U.S. and also elevate sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: Ezra D Romero

item.title: What is an aortic dissection?
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with American Heart Association's volunteer president Dr. Manesh Patel about aortic dissection, the preliminary finding of what led to Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with American Heart Association's volunteer president Dr. Manesh Patel about aortic dissection, the preliminary finding of what led to Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: France's Bastille Day celebrations dampened by heatwave and raging wildfires
item.description: As France celebrates Bastille Day, its soccer team is facing off against Spain in the World Cup and the country is enduring its third intense heatwave this year, with forest fires raging in the south and outside Paris.
item.content:encoded: As France celebrates Bastille Day, its soccer team is facing off against Spain in the World Cup and the country is enduring its third intense heatwave this year, with forest fires raging in the south and outside Paris.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Bloch
